TSLA.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on with near-term caveats. S&P 500 at fresh all-time highs above 7,200, best April since 2020. Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% but 4 dissents and Powell succession (May 15 Kevin Warsh) introduce policy uncertainty. Iran peace talks are the dominant catalyst shift — WTI -2% reduces energy inflation tail risk. Net regime read: risk-on but with transitional policy uncertainty that caps conviction. This is broadly supportive of growth names but not a clean regime signal. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: TSLA is UNDERPERFORMING the market. Daily ROC: 5d +2.12%, 10d -1.87%, 21d +2.66%. S&P 500 had its best April since 2020 (~5-8% month), implying TSLA's 21d +2.66% significantly lags the broad market. Price remains below its 50 EMA (-1.09%), 200 EMA (-1.96%), and 200 SMA (-5.1%). The broader market is making all-time highs while TSLA cannot clear its 50 EMA. Sector headwind confirmed. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R MATH: Current price ~$396. Nearest daily resistance: $409.28 (15 tests) = +3.4% upside. Stop below daily support at $381.40 / entry stop ~$378 = -4.5% downside. R/R to first target: 3.4% / 4.5% = 0.76:1 — fails 2:1 minimum by a wide margin. Extending target to $416.38 (second resistance, 12 tests): 5.1% / 4.5% = 1.1:1 — still fails. Fib ext 1.272 at $428.87 = +8.3% upside: 8.3% / 4.5% = 1.85:1 — still below the 2:1 minimum. No entry point clears the asymmetry bar. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: (a) Hourly RSI at 70.5 — overbought, price has surged 4.1-5.7% above hourly EMAs in today's session (rel vol 2.74x). This is a late-entry signal, not early sector rotation. (b) Daily OBV trend is DOWN despite price recovery — consistent with distribution into the rally by larger participants. (c) TSLA thesis split (EV deterioration vs autonomy premium) creates fundamental uncertainty; wiki notes the stock was un-tradeable for swing purposes until Q1 earnings (April 22) resolved margin ambiguity. Post-earnings the setup is clearer but relative weakness vs market persists. (d) LESSONS: Recent NVDA EMA pullback Fib dip-buy lost -$264.65 when stop triggered at stretched macro (RSI 81-87). Hourly RSI at 70.5 today on TSLA puts this in a similar stretched zone. The macro context that failed NVDA (overextended intraday + macro RSI stretch) is partially analogous here. CONCLUSION: Macro tailwind is real but TSLA is not participating — it is underperforming while the market makes all-time highs. The intraday surge is overbought (hourly RSI 70.5, price 5%+ above hourly EMAs) right into a heavily-tested resistance cluster at $409. R/R fails at every target level. OBV distribution on daily. Wind is not fully at this trade's back despite the risk-on regime. Pass.
